title: Indulgent Korean Tortilla Jjigae
description: A nutty, aerated Korean stew that marries crispy toasted tortillas with a savory gochujang broth, festive kimchi, and fresh vegetables, served hot in a traditional pot
instructions: Cut tortillas into 1-inch squares and arrange on a sheet to dry for 5 minutes.
Heat a small skillet over medium heat and lightly brush each tortilla square with sesame oil.
Place the tortilla squares in the skillet and toast until golden brown and slightly crisp, about 2 minutes per side.
In a separate pot, combine vegetable broth, gochujang, soy sauce, sugar, garlic, onion, carrot, zucchini, tofu, and kimchi and bring to a gentle boil.
Add the toasted tortilla squares to the simmering broth and cook for 5 minutes to allow flavors to meld.
Stir in honey to create a subtle caramel glaze over the broth.
Reduce heat to low and simmer for an additional 5 minutes so the tortilla absorbs the broth.
Garnish with sliced green onions and toasted sesame seeds before serving.
Serve hot in a traditional hot‑pot vessel for an authentic experience.
Enjoy the nutty aroma and festive flavors with family or friends.
